title: HTML Tokenization, Tree Construction, goquery, and Parser Differentials · Go Semantics Case: Token Vs Tree
summary: Source markup is treated as browser DOM, tokenizer and tree APIs are mixed, goquery is assumed to run JavaScript, malformed HTML repair is never tested. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go token vs tree increment in budgeted Go HTML tree adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.
Keep raw, decoded, tree, main-content, record, and rendered digests separate with algorithm and version.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Token Vs Tree · Theory Token Vs Tree
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5981 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove tokenizer versus tree choice, goquery documents, malformed repair, browser differentials, and budgets; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.

Build deterministic Go evidence to prove tokenizer versus tree choice, goquery documents, malformed repair, browser differentials, and budgets; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Token Vs Tree model · Theory Token Vs Tree, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Tokenizer and DOM tree APIs expose equivalent information and cost.
